topic.name: Federal Legislative Actions
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various federal legislative measures addressing funding transparency, conservation efforts, veterans' assistance, economic stabilization, and energy management.

Document 1: Transparency in Federal Funding Act of 2007 - Requires disclosure of earmarked funds to Congress.
Document 2: Establishes a Rare Cats and Canids Conservation Fund for conservation efforts.
Document 4: Regulates housing assistance for post-9/11 veterans pursuing distance learning.
Document 7: Amends the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act regarding asset purchases.
Document 9: Amends the Energy Independence and Security Act to require refinery outage reporting.

topic.name: Medicare Policy Reforms
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at reforming and improving Medicare services and coverage.

Document 1: Integrity in Medicare Advanced Diagnostic Imaging Act of 2009
prohibits a physician from referring a patient to the entity for certain advanced diagnostic imaging services
Document 2: Physician Pathology Services Continuity Act of 2011
require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to treat such component as a service for which payment shall be made to the laboratory
Document 3: Medicare Skilled Nursing and Home Health Services Continuity of Care Act of 2015
ensure beneficiary access to advance wound care products while in a skilled nursing facility
Document 4: Safeguarding Our Hospitals' Futures Act of 2008
to continue to apply budget neutrality on a national basis in the calculation of the urban hospital wage floor
Document 5: Medicare Prescription Drug Price Negotiation Act of 2015
to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to negotiate with pharmaceutical manufacturers the prices
Document 6: MAC Transparency Act
require each contract entered into with a prescription drug plan sponsor to prohibit the PDP from entering into a contract with any pharmacy benefits manager
Document 7: Helping Ensure Life- and Limb-Saving Access to Podiatric Physicians Act
to include podiatrists as physicians in order to cover their services under the Medicaid program
Document 8: Virtual Screening for Cancer Act of 2007
provide Medicare coverage for screening computed tomography as a colorectal screening test
Document 9: to repeal the exclusion from medical assistance under the Medicaid program of items and services for patients in an institution for mental diseases
Document 10: Preventive Services Medicare Parity Act of 2016
provide for Medicare coverage of certain preventive services for which coverage is required by group and individual health insurance plans

topic.name: Education Data and Improvement Programs
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at enhancing educational outcomes through data utilization, funding, and support systems for schools and educators.

Document 1: improve access to, and the sharing and use of, education data to improve student outcomes
build the capacity of teachers, leaders, parents, students, researchers, and the public to use longitudinal data for effective decision-making.
Document 2: expand the loan forgiveness available to public elementary and secondary school teachers
raises to $25,000 the amount of the FFEL or DL outstanding that may be forgiven after a public school teacher's fifth complete school year of teaching.
Document 5: implement a college-preparatory curriculum aligned with rigorous secondary school studies
provide each student with an academic teacher advisor with whom the student regularly meets.
Document 8: assist public elementary or secondary schools to function as full-service community schools
provide access to such services to students, families, and the community.
Document 9: improve the academic achievement of elementary and secondary school students
increasing individualized learning time or extended learning programs.

topic.name: Temporary Tariff Suspensions
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States that involve temporary suspensions or reductions of duties on various goods.

Document 1: reduce temporarily the duty on lithium ion electrical storage batteries.
Document 2: extend the temporary suspension of duty on filament tow of rayon.
Document 3: suspend temporarily the duty on certain structures, parts, and components for use in an isotopic separation facility.
Document 4: extend the temporary reduction of duty on certain polyacrylonitrile tow.
Document 5: extend the temporary suspension of duty on camel hair, not processed.
Document 6: suspend temporarily the duty on certain men's or boys' knit-to-shape wool vests.
Document 7: suspend temporarily the duty on women's belts of leather or composition leather.
Document 8: extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain chemicals.
Document 9: suspend temporarily the duty on mixtures of cobaltate.
Document 10: temporarily suspend the duty on 1,2-Propanediol and related compounds.

topic.name: Veterans Benefits Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative acts aimed at improving benefits and support for veterans, particularly concerning education, disability compensation, and healthcare services.

Document 1: Veterans Education Tax Security Act of 2015
excludes from the gross income of a veteran with a service-connected disability
Document 2: Veteran Vocational Rehabilitation and Employment Subsistence Allowance Improvement Act of 2009
increases the amount of monthly subsistence allowance payable to veterans
Document 3: COMBAT PTSD Act
defines 'combat with the enemy' for veterans' disability compensation
Document 4: reduces the level of difficulty required in the use of prostheses
additional veterans' disability compensation for service-disabled veterans
Document 5: Mandatory Veteran Specialist Training Act of 2009
reduces the period for training program completion
Document 6: Providing Leadership and Improving Veterans Care Act
prohibits VA senior executive bonuses without filling vacant positions
Document 7: Continuing Care for Veterans Act of 2014
prohibits altering health care available to enrolled veterans
Document 9: Veterans Emergency Health Care Safety Net Expansion Act of 2014
eliminates requirement limiting reimbursement for emergency treatment
Document 10: Veterans Backlog Reduction Act
directs VA to pay provisional benefits for unadjudicated claims

topic.name: Oil and Gas Regulation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the exploration, production, and environmental safety measures related to oil and gas resources in the United States.

1: Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to revise provisions concerning ultra-deepwater and unconventional natural gas and other petroleum resources.

Directs the Secretary of Energy (DOE) to establish a program of awards to support the development, demonstration, and commercialization of innovative technologies to prevent, stop, or capture large-scale accidental discharges of oil.
2: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from authorizing any new lease for exploration or production of oil or natural gas unless the lessee certifies for each existing lease that the lessee has diligently developed the lands.
Instructs the Secretary to promulgate diligent development regulations that include benchmarks for oil and gas development.
3: Expresses the sense of Congress that the President should stop filling the Strategic Petroleum Reserve until the price of petroleum no longer has a major adverse impact upon the national economy.
Amends the Clean Air Act to authorize the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to give preference to approval of state implementation plan provisions.
5: Revises the policy concerning resource development on the outer Continental Shelf (OCS) to include emphasis on the importance of protection, maintenance, and restoration of the marine and coastal environments.

Authorizes the leasing, exploration, and development of oil and gas resources of the OCS only if the Secretary of the Interior has a thorough understanding of the marine coastal environments affected by the activity.

topic.name: Immigration Policy Reforms
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative efforts aimed at reforming immigration laws and policies in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA) to provide increased protections and eligibility for family-sponsored immigrants.
Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to deny a family-based immigration petition by a U.S. petitioner for an alien spouse or child if the petitioner is on the national sex offender registry.
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to make up to 55,000 visas available in FY2014 and subsequent fiscal years to qualified immigrants who have a doctorate degree in a STEM field.
Defines 'United States doctoral institution of higher education' as an institution that is defined under the Higher Education Act of 1965.
Document 3: Requires that, in addition to the Department of Homeland Security screening, the Federal Bureau of Investigation shall take all actions necessary to ensure that each covered alien receives a background investigation before U.S. refugee admission.
A 'covered alien' is any alien applying for U.S. refugee admission who is a national or resident of Iraq or Syria.
Document 4: Amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996 to allow employers to use the employment eligibility confirmation system to verify the identity and employment eligibility of an employee from the time of application for employment.
Document 5: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to authorize a Department of Homeland Security official to waive certain entry documentary requirements for a nonimmigrant child who is a citizen or national of Mexico.
Sets forth additional requirements for such entries.
Document 6: Amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996 to make a sanctuary city ineligible to receive certain federal funds.
The Attorney General shall prioritize a request from the Secretary of Homeland Security to transfer an alien who is without lawful status.
Document 7: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to increase employer civil fines for hiring an alien that the employer knows is not authorized for employment.
Sets forth a federal-state allocation of such additional penalty funds.
Document 8: Suspends the visa waiver program and adjustment to permanent resident status.
Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to establish a public listing of every alien subject to a final order of deportation.
Document 9: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to consider a person born in the United States 'subject to the jurisdiction' of the United States for citizenship at birth purposes if the person is born in the United States of certain parents.
Defines eligibility for citizenship at birth.
Document 10: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to carry out a Criminal Alien Program to identify incarcerated criminal aliens.
Makes a state that does not cooperate with the Secretary ineligible for Department of Justice law enforcement grant programs.

topic.name: EMP Weapons Accountability
topic.description: Legislation focused on the oversight and reporting of foreign electromagnetic pulse weapons programs.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.

topic.name: Wilderness Designation and Management
topic.description: The documents discuss the designation, management, and boundary adjustments of various wilderness areas within the National Wilderness Preservation System.

Document 1: Designates certain lands in Rocky Mountain National Park... as a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Sets forth provisions governing continued operations at the Grand River Ditch.
Document 3: Designates certain lands in Rocky Mountain National Park... as a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Prohibits any use of motorized vehicles and other motorized machinery in the Area that was not permitted on March 1, 2006.
Document 5: Designates certain Bureau of Land Management (BLM) land in Oregon as wilderness...
Requires any land or interest in land within the boundary of the Oregon Badlands Wilderness that is acquired by the United States to become part of the Wilderness.
Document 7: Designates specified federal land managed by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM)... as wilderness and as a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to take necessary measures in the wilderness designated by this Act to control fire, insects, and diseases.

topic.name: Child Welfare and Protection
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative measures aimed at enhancing child welfare, preventing child abuse, and addressing issues related to child trafficking and exploitation.

Document 1: Strengthening Child Welfare Response to Trafficking Act of 2015
condition eligibility to receive a state grant for child abuse or neglect prevention and treatment programs
Document 2: Arresting Child Predators Act of 2010
extend the authority for issuing administrative subpoenas in cases involving the sexual exploitation or abuse of children
Document 3: Speak Out to Stop Child Abuse Act
require the state plan under a grant from the Secretary of Health and Human Services for child abuse and neglect prevention and treatment programs to contain an assurance
Document 4: Elder Justice Act of 2009
establish an Elder Justice program under title XX
Document 5: Fostering Connections to Success and Increasing Adoptions Act of 2008
provide kinship guardianship assistance payments to grandparents and other relatives
Document 6: Investment in Kids' Instruction, Development and Support Act
revise requirements for foster care and adoption assistance and for child welfare services
Document 8: Stand Against Violence and Empower Native Women Act
include sex trafficking as a target of the grants to Indian tribal governments to combat violent crime against Indian women
Document 9: SMART Teen Dating Violence Awareness and Prevention Act
authorize grants for the purpose of preventing domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, and stalking
Document 10: Violence Against Women Reauthorization Act of 2012
amend the Violence Against Women Act to add or expand definitions of several terms for purposes of such Act

topic.name: Mental Health Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative efforts aimed at improving mental health services, training, and prevention across different populations including military personnel, children, and the elderly.

Document 1: Directs the Department of Defense and the Department of Veterans Affairs to ensure that all health care providers receive empirically supported training on the recognition and assessment of individuals at risk for suicide.
Requires such Departments to conduct annual evaluations of the implementation by mental health care providers under their jurisdictions.
Document 2: Amends the Older Americans Act to ensure that all programs funded include appropriate training in elder abuse prevention.
Develop a National Adult Protective Services Resources Center.
Document 3: Amends the Public Health Service Act to expand the focus of a youth interagency research, training, and technical assistance resource center from youth suicides to suicide at any age.
Revises and extends grant programs for the development of state or tribal youth suicide early intervention and prevention strategies.
Document 4: Amends the Public Health Service Act to authorize the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish programs related to mental health services for children and adolescents.
Awards scholarships to students who agree to work as child and adolescent mental health service professionals.
Document 5: Authorizes the use of specified funds to provide housing counseling and financial counseling for individuals with mental illness.
Includes in a state plan for comprehensive community-based mental health systems support services for individuals released from inpatient or residential institutions.
Document 6: Sets forth criteria for the certification of federally-qualified community behavioral health centers.
Authorizes the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award matching grants to states or Indian tribes for community-based mental health services.
Document 7: Requires the Director of the National Institutes of Health to expand, intensify, and coordinate eating disorder research.
Establishes the Interagency Eating Disorders Coordinating Council within the Department of Health and Human Services.
Document 8: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants to improve mental and behavioral health services on college campuses.
Directs the Secretary to establish the College Campus Task Force to discuss mental and behavioral health concerns on college campuses.

topic.name: FDA Drug Regulation and Safety
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the regulation, approval, and safety of pharmaceuticals and medical devices by the FDA.

Document 9: Establishes the Physician Payment Update Commission to study and report on payment rates under the Medicare physician fee schedule.
Document 10: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to impose additional penalties for cultivating or manufacturing controlled substances on federal property.

topic.name: Water Resource Management
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative acts aimed at the restoration, management, and enhancement of water resources and ecosystems in different regions of the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior...
to participate in the design, planning, and construction of an advanced water treatment plant facility and recycled water system.
Document 2: Authorizes, ratifies, and confirms the Big Sandy River-Planet Ranch Water Rights Settlement Agreement...
Directs the Secretary of Interior to execute both agreements.
Document 3: Directs the Chief of Engineers, in coordination with Los Angeles County, California, to prepare a feasibility study for environmental ecosystem restoration...
identify demonstration projects for construction and restoration of the San Gabriel River Watershed.
Document 4: Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to carry out the project for ecosystem restoration, water supply, flood control, and protection of water quality...
in accordance with the recommendations of the report of the Chief of Engineers.
Document 5: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior...
to participate in the planning, design, and construction of water recycling facilities.
Document 6: Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to carry out the project for ecosystem restoration, water supply, flood control, and protection of water quality...
in accordance with the recommendations of the report of the Chief of Engineers.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of the Interior, in coordination with Los Angeles County, California, to prepare a feasibility study for environmental ecosystem restoration...
identify demonstration projects for construction and restoration of the San Gabriel River Watershed.
Document 8: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior...
to participate in the planning, design, and construction of water recycling facilities.

topic.name: Mortgage Regulation and Consumer Protection
topic.description: This cluster addresses various legislative measures aimed at regulating mortgage lending practices and protecting consumers in the mortgage market.

Document 1: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to limit to a city or town with under 50,000 inhabitants the meaning of 'rural' with respect to rural lenders.
Presume that the applicant for a residential mortgage loan has a reasonable ability to repay the loan.
Document 2: Preserving Consumer's Mortgage Origination Choices Act of 2012 - Amends the Truth in Lending Act with respect to the prohibitions against steering incentives.
Allows a mortgage originator, at the consumer's request, to reduce the amount of compensation the originator is receiving.
Document 3: Mortgage Finance Act of 2015 - Appoints the Federal Housing Finance Agency (FHFA) receiver of the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae).
Establishes the MFA as an independent agency of the federal government to guarantee securities issued by qualified issuers.
Document 4: Community Lending Enhancement and Regulatory Relief Act of 2015 - Amends the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act to exempt from its annual privacy policy notice requirement.
Directs the federal banking agencies to study jointly the appropriate capital requirements for mortgage servicing assets.
Document 5: Regulation of Mortgage Servicing Act of 2013 - Requires a mortgage servicer to assign one case manager to each borrower that seeks a mortgage loan modification.
Prohibits a servicer from initiating a covered foreclosure action against a borrower unless specified tasks have been performed.
Document 6: Rebuilding Equity Act of 2012 - Requires Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac to establish a voluntary program for eligible borrowers.
GSEs shall pay up to $1,000 of the closing costs associated with applying for refinancing.
Document 7: Reverse Mortgage Stabilization Act of 2013 - Amends the National Housing Act with respect to mortgage insurance for home equity conversion mortgages.
Authorize the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to establish additional requirements for the reverse mortgage program.
Document 8: Prompt Notification of Short Sales Act - Requires each servicer of a home mortgage to provide prompt notifications regarding short sale requests.
An aggrieved individual may bring a civil action for equitable relief and a monetary award of $1,000.
Document 9: Negative Amortization Mortgage Loan Transparency Act - Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require specified prior disclosures to a first-time borrower.
Requires the consumer to provide documentation of receiving home ownership counseling.
Document 10: Emergency Homeownership and Equity Protection Act - Amends federal bankruptcy law governing a Chapter 13 debtor.
Allows modification of the rights of claim holders in the event of a foreclosure notice.

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at the conservation, protection, and restoration of coral reef ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects.
Authorizes actions to minimize injury to a coral reef or loss of an ecosystem function resulting from human activities.
Document 2: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to transfer certain authority from the Administrator of NOAA to the Secretary of Commerce.
Directs the Secretary to submit to Congress a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy, revised as necessary.
Document 3: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to direct the Secretary of Commerce to make grants for projects for the conservation of coral reef ecosystems.
Authorizes the Secretary to conduct activities to conserve coral reef ecosystems.
Document 4: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to transfer certain authority from NOAA to the Secretary of Commerce.
Directs the Secretary to give priority to community-based local action strategies when awarding certain grants for conservation projects.
Document 5: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects.
Establishes the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force to coordinate federal actions that are designed to preserve and protect coral reef ecosystems.
Document 6: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects.
Modifies the goals and objectives of the national coral reef action strategy.
Document 7: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds.
Authorizes the Administrator of NOAA to undertake actions to minimize injury to a coral reef from vessel impacts.
Document 8: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to direct the Secretary of Commerce to submit to specified congressional committees a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy.
Authorizes the Secretary to make agreements with nonprofit organizations for public-private partnerships promoting coral reef ecosystem conservation.
Document 9: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to expand the coral reef conservation program by awarding remaining program grant funds to appropriate projects.
Directs NOAA to submit to Congress a national coral reef action strategy.
Document 10: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act to authorize the 12 federal agencies on the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force to carry out prize competitions that promote coral reef research and conservation.
Priority is given to establish programs that address communities, environments, or industries that are in distress due to the decline or degradation of coral reef ecosystems.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Assistance
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts related to agricultural support, food security, and nutrition programs in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 to authorize the Secretary of Agriculture to consider national average market price variations for different classes of wheat when determining wheat producer eligibility for 2007 counter-cyclical payments.
States that in calculating a separate effective price for a class of wheat the effective price shall equal the sum of...
Document 2: Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 to suspend the limitation on the period for which borrowers are eligible for loan guarantee assistance through December 31, 2013.
Document 3: Amends the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act to make foster children categorically eligible for free lunches and breakfasts under the school lunch and breakfast programs.
Document 4: Amends the Agricultural Adjustment Act to direct the Secretary of Agriculture to base the minimum Class II milk price on the average production cost of producing all milk in the 48 contiguous states.
States that in the case of a federal milk order, disapproval of an amendment to the order shall not be considered disapproval of the...
Document 5: States that it is U.S. policy to promote global food security, improve agricultural productivity, and support the development of institutions of higher learning.
Directs the President to report to the appropriate congressional committees annually through 2014 regarding such strategy's implementation.
Document 6: Amends the U.S. Troop Readiness, Veterans' Care, Katrina Recovery, and Iraq Accountability Appropriations Act, 2007 to eliminate the requirement that forage producers must participate in the insurance pilot program or the noninsured crop disaster assistance program in order to receive crop disaster and livestock assistance.
Document 7: Amends the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act to replace the farm-to-cafeteria program with a program providing schools, state and local agencies...
Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to promote the use of salad or fruit and vegetable bars in schools participating in the school lunch program.
Document 8: Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 regarding the biobased markets program to extend program funding through FY2018.
Provides for a program of biobased product education and promotion activities.
Document 9: Amends the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act to allow schools and local educational agencies to receive special assistance payments for free or reduced price meals...
Requires schools and LEAs reimbursed on the basis of estimates or certifications to provide free meals to all their students under the school lunch and breakfast programs.
Document 10: Provides for the inclusion of locally or regionally produced agricultural food products under specified agricultural, rural development, agricultural research, crop insurance, and nutrition programs.
Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to establish a wireless retailer electronic benefit transfer pilot program for smartphone technology.

topic.name: Renewable Energy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at regulating and promoting renewable energy sources and biofuels in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Clean Air Act to revise the renewable fuel program
revoking the renewable fuel standard for 2013-2022
Document 2: Sets forth a schedule for motor vehicle fuel to contain specified volumes of renewable fuel
increases funding for bioenergy research and development
Document 3: Authorizes federal acquisition of green technologies
encouraging the use of sustainable biomass, wind, small-scale hydroelectric, solar, geothermal
Document 4: Deems the Draft Proposed Outer Continental Shelf Oil and Gas Leasing Program 2010-2015 approved
requires the Secretary to issue a final leasing plan for the Eastern Gulf of Mexico
Document 5: Requires the Secretary of Energy to establish a cooperative industry-government research and development program
to demonstrate novel and innovative technologies to capture or prevent carbon dioxide emissions
Document 6: amends the Clean Air Act to revise the renewable fuel standard program
the renewable fuel that is required to be blended into gasoline must be advanced biofuel
Document 7: Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to include projects for infrastructure for provision and distribution of alternative fuels
develop a methodology for calculating the equivalent volumes of conventional fuel displaced by use of each alternative fuel
Document 8: requires the Department of Energy to integrate water considerations into its energy research
advancing energy and energy efficiency technologies and practices
Document 9: Prohibits the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency from regulating carbon dioxide emissions
until the Secretary of Commerce certifies that China, India, and Russia have initiated measures
Document 10: Instructs the Secretary of Energy to make grants to implement renewable energy projects
that generate electricity from solar, wind, geothermal, or ocean energy

topic.name: Data Privacy and Security Legislation
topic.description: A collection of legislative acts focused on enhancing the protection of personally identifiable information and establishing penalties for data breaches and unauthorized access.

Document 1: Amends provisions of the Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act of 1974 that prohibit the Department of Education from funding educational agencies or institutions that release student educational records

Prohibits students' educational records or personally identifiable information from being released by an authorized representative to any individual, agency, or organization, other than the official who has direct control over such representative.
Document 2: Amends federal criminal code provisions relating to computer fraud and unauthorized access to computers
Requires federal agencies as part of their rulemaking process to prepare and make available to the public privacy impact assessments.
Document 3: Imposes a fine and/or prison term of up to five years for intentionally or willfully concealing a security breach

Makes it unlawful for a service provider to knowingly or intentionally redirect web searches or otherwise monitor, manipulate, aggregate, and market data from websites without the consent of the Internet user.
Document 4: Requires timely notification to individuals whose sensitive personal information could be compromised as a result of a breach
Makes it the duty of each agency Chief Human Capital Officer to prescribe policies and procedures for employee exit interviews.
Document 7: Defines 'sensitive personally identifiable information' to include specified combinations of data elements in electronic or digital form
Imposes a prison term of up to five years and/or a fine on any individual who has knowledge of and intentionally and willfully conceals a security breach.
Document 8: Prohibits a nongovernmental individual or entity from knowingly collecting, obtaining, or disclosing geolocation information without express authorization
Authorizes civil actions by the Attorney General, state attorneys general, and aggrieved individuals for violations of this Act.
Document 9: Requires the Department of Health and Human Services to notify an individual within two business days after discovery of any breach of security of any system maintained by a health care exchange
known to have resulted in the theft or unlawful access of the individual's personally identifiable information.
Document 10: Amends the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009 to expand requirements for reports to Congress on acquisition or disclosure of unsecured protected health information

Requires the Attorney General to report annually to Congress, and to publish, a study of complaints of alleged violations concerning wrongful disclosure of individually identifiable health information.

topic.name: Tax Fraud and Identity Theft Prevention
topic.description: Legislation aimed at preventing tax fraud and identity theft through enhanced penalties, law enforcement coordination, and taxpayer protection measures.

Document 1: impose a fine and/or prison term on any person who knowingly or willfully misappropriates another person's tax identification number
require the Commissioner of Internal Revenue to submit to the Senate Committee on Finance and the House Committee on Ways and Means an annual report on the number of reported cases of tax fraud
Document 2: disseminate to seniors and their families and caregivers information on mail, telemarketing, and Internet fraud targeting seniors
establish and administer a competitive grant program for mail, telemarketing, and Internet fraud prevention education programs for senior citizens
Document 3: extend to eight years the statute of limitations for certain public corruption offenses
increase the civil and criminal penalties for improper disclosure or use of taxpayer information by tax return preparers
Document 4: establish legal presumptions against the validity of transactions involving offshore secrecy jurisdictions
require tax withholding agents and financial institutions to report certain information about beneficial owners of foreign-owned financial accounts
Document 5: authorize the Secretary of the Treasury to impose restrictions on foreign jurisdictions or financial institutions
increase penalties for promoting abusive tax shelters and for aiding and abetting the understatement of tax liability
Document 6: increase civil penalties for willful violations of anti-money laundering laws
impose a 20-year maximum prison term for individuals who facilitate evasion of an anti-money laundering program
Document 7: authorize the Secretary to disclose tax return information to federal, state, and local law enforcement personnel
require the Commissioner of the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) to report to Congress on the number of reported tax fraud cases
Document 9: authorize the Secretary of the Treasury to disclose tax return information to federal and state law enforcement agencies
implement an identity theft tax fraud prevention program

topic.name: Banking Regulation and Oversight
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative measures aimed at regulating and overseeing banking institutions and financial entities to ensure stability and prevent systemic risks.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (FDIA) to prohibit an insured depository institution from being an affiliate of any broker or dealer, investment adviser, investment company, or any other person or entity engaged principally in the issue, flotation, underwriting, public sale, or distribution of stocks, bonds, debentures, notes, or other securities.
Prohibits officers, directors and employees of securities firms from simultaneous service on the boards of depository institutions.
Document 2: Directs the Inspector General (IG) of the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) to study the impact of the failure of insured depository institutions.
Requires the study to examine the impact of significant losses arising from loans for which all payments were current on the ability of insured depository institutions to raise additional capital.
Document 3: Amends the Truth in Lending Act (TILA), with respect to minimum standards for residential mortgage loans and the presumption of ability to repay, to treat as a 'qualified mortgage' any residential mortgage loan made by a creditor having less than $10 billion in total assets.
Directs the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) to exempt a creditor with assets of less than $10 billion from the requirement to establish escrow or impound accounts.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Reserve Act (FRA) to direct the Chairman of the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) to submit to the Comptroller General (GAO) and to certain congressional committees a Directive Policy Rule (DPR).
Establishes a one-week blackout period during which permissible public communications by FOMC members and staff are restricted.
Document 5: Amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act (Dodd-Frank) concerning establishment of minimum leverage and minimum risk-based capital requirements on a consolidated basis for a depository institution holding company.

States that federal banking agencies shall not be required to subject any person to such minimum capital requirements, to the extent that such person either acts in its capacity as a regulated insurance entity.

topic.name: Wildlife Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures aimed at the conservation and protection of wildlife, particularly migratory birds and polar bears, while also addressing hunting and recreational activities.

Document 1: Amends the Migratory Bird Hunting and Conservation Stamp Act
Prohibits such amount from exceeding $25 for the first 5-year period

Document 3: Requires the Secretary of the Interior to report to Congress
Amends the Toxic Substances Control Act to exclude certain firearms components
Document 4: Directs the Department of Agriculture to implement authorized wildfire prevention projects
Requires projects carried out on threatened and endangered species habitat
Document 5: Requires a federal public land management official to facilitate hunting
Permits closures or restrictions on such land for purposes including resource conservation
Document 6: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from offering or approving any new lease
The polar bear is listed as either an endangered or threatened species
Document 7: Amends the Marine Mammal Protection Act to allow the importation of polar bear parts
Taken legally in sport hunts in Canada
Document 8: Determination by the United States Fish and Wildlife Service of the threatened status of the polar bear
Shall have no force or effect
Document 9: Requires the Administrator of each of the Bonneville Power Administration to include estimates of compliance costs
Submit an annual report estimating such costs
Document 10: Amends the Marine Mammal Protection Act of 1972
To allow the importation of polar bear parts taken legally in sport hunts in Canada

topic.name: Legislative Measures and Reforms
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative measures and reforms aimed at addressing social, environmental, and administrative issues in the United States.

Document 1: Helping Expand Lending Practices in Rural Communities Act
Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) to establish an application process
Document 2: Breast Cancer Research Stamp Reauthorization Act of 2015
requiring the U.S. Postal Service to issue a special postage stamp

Requires the Secretary to coordinate with relevant federal departments and agencies to ensure that environmental reviews are made in a manner that accelerates delivery of such projects.
Document 3: Reauthorizes the Great Lakes Fish and Wildlife Restoration Act and revises requirements for funding requests to restore fish and wildlife resources and habitat.
Focuses on regional projects that restore the fish and wildlife resources and habitat of the Great Lakes Basin.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to establish a national container fee to be collected on each cargo container imported into, or exported from, a covered U.S. seaport.
Establishes the National Goods Movement Improvement Grant Program to provide grant funding for eligible projects, including environmental projects located at or near a covered U.S. seaport.
Document 7: Amends the Nonindigenous Aquatic Nuisance Prevention and Control Act to require certain vessels to have invasive species management plans.
Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to establish vessel performance requirements with the goal of eliminating invasive species.

topic.name: Innovative Infrastructure Development
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at enhancing infrastructure through innovative technologies and collaborative programs across various sectors including construction, education, and environmental management.

Document 1: This bill directs the Department of Agriculture (USDA) to establish a performance-driven research and development program to advance tall wood building construction in the United States.
USDA shall carry out a program of education and technical assistance for mass timber applications.
Document 2: The President is authorized to establish programs to support cooperation, training, and mentoring of entrepreneurs in the covered region.

Priority should be given to strengthening ties between communities and academic institutions in those portions of the United States and Mexico that are within 100 kilometers of the international boundary between those countries.
Document 5: Amends the Federal Power Act to direct the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to designate one or more regions within the Eastern Interconnection and the Western Interconnection to be represented by specified regional transmission planners.

Requires the federal power marketing administrations and transmitting utilities in a designated region to integrate their transmission plans with the regional transmission plans required under this Act.

topic.name: Federal Legislative Acts
topic.description: A collection of federal legislative acts addressing various aspects of governance, including regulatory reforms, military authority, veterans' healthcare, and interstate commerce.

Document 1: Interstate Recognition of Notarizations Act of 2007
Requires each federal and state court to recognize any lawful notarization occurring in or affecting interstate commerce.
Document 2: National Guard Empowerment Act of 2007
Expands the authority of the Chief of the National Guard Bureau.
Document 3: Chiropractic Care Available to All Veterans Act
Requires a program under which the Secretary of Veterans Affairs provides chiropractic care and services to veterans.
Document 4: College Cost Reduction Act of 2012
Directs the Secretary of Education to establish the Higher Education Regulatory Reform Task Force.
Document 5: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act
Limits the somatic cell count in all dairy products sold in interstate commerce.
Document 6: Chiropractic Care Available to All Veterans Act
Requires a program under which the Secretary of Veterans Affairs provides chiropractic care and services to veterans.
Document 7: Authorizes a faculty member of a military service academy to secure federal copyright protection
Requires the faculty member to transfer such copyright to the owner or publisher.
Document 8: Regulatory Reform Act of 2012
Directs the Administrator of the Office of Information and Regulatory Affairs to post online a Federal Regulatory Reform Report.
Document 9: Success in Countering Al Qaeda Reporting Requirements Act of 2009
Directs the President to submit annual counterterrorism reports.
Document 10: Veterans Health Care Budget Reform Act of 2008
Requires a two-fiscal-year budget authority for the Department of Veterans Affairs.

topic.name: Federal Legislative Initiatives
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various federal legislative acts aimed at improving education, energy technology, and accountability in government spending.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Energy to develop and publish near-, medium-, and long-term goals for the deployment of clean energy technologies.
Requires annual reports to Congress on the technologies supported and on Corporation performance.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to award grants to states to create or approve teacher or principal preparation academies.
Requires each academy to provide enrollees with a significant portion of their training through clinical preparation.
Document 3: Amends the school improvement program to require states to include information on military-connected students in their annual report cards.
How those students performed in comparison to all their other students on the assessments of student progress.
Document 4: Prohibits the Chairman of the Nuclear Regulatory Commission from exercising emergency authority unless certain conditions are met.
Prescribes additional procedures if the Chairman does declare such emergency exists.
Document 5: Directs the National Council on Disability to report to Congress with recommendations to improve the implementation of the Americans with Disabilities Act.
Recommendations focus on travel, tourism, and hospitality industries.
Document 6: Amends the National and Community Service Act of 1990 to revise their programs and reauthorize appropriations.
Involves program participants in emergency and disaster preparedness, relief, and recovery.
Document 7: Directs the Department of Commerce to issue orders applicable to manufacturers of concrete masonry products.
Establishes a Concrete Masonry Products Board to carry out a program of generic promotion, research, and education.
Document 8: Requires the head of a federal agency to implement all recommendations of the agency's inspector general regarding wasteful spending.
Prohibits an agency from obligating funds if it fails to implement an IG recommendation in a timely manner.
Document 9: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act to allow states to use academic indicators other than standardized assessments.
Permits states to use growth or hybrid growth models in determining student progress.
Document 10: Establishes the Corporate Subsidy Reform Commission to examine federal programs providing inequitable subsidies.
Requires the head of each federal department to include a list of programs providing inequitable federal subsidies in budget justification documents.

topic.name: Regulatory Acts and Management Plans
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts aimed at regulating specific sectors, including environmental management, fisheries, healthcare, education, and financial institutions.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to allow members of the Hoonah Indian Association to collect the eggs of glaucous-winged gulls
Considers the collection of those eggs within the Park by the Association to be a use specifically permitted by the Alaska National Interest Lands Conservation Act.
Document 2: Directs the Mid-Atlantic Fishery Management Council to submit for approval by the Secretary of Commerce a modified fishery management plan
Requires the Council, in preparing such modifications or amendments, to consult with the Atlantic States Marine Fisheries Commission.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Labor to allow any employer in interstate commerce that is required by federal or state law to pay a minimum wage rate higher than the current federal rate
to include the value of creditable health care benefits in determining the required wage.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow individual taxpayers a refundable tax credit for the cost of obtaining travel documents necessary to comply with the terrorist travel and effective screening plan
Document 5: Instructs the Secretary of Energy to implement a program to award cash prizes competitively for research, development, demonstration, and commercial application of nuclear used fuel storage.
Authorizes the Secretary to enter into an agreement with a private, nonprofit entity to administer the prize competition.
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow wrongfully incarcerated individuals an exclusion from gross income for compensation received relating to their incarceration
Defines 'wrongfully incarcerated individual' as an individual who was convicted of a criminal offense and was then pardoned or found not guilty.
Document 7: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to revise requirements relating to annual yearly progress (AYP) of students and schools
Authorizes the Secretary of Education to modify an AYP timeline.
Document 8: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to allow borrowers of Direct Stafford Loans to opt to extend the period before they must begin to repay such loans
Subsidizes the interest that accrues on Direct Stafford Loans for the first six months of that extended grace period.
Document 9: Authorizes the Assistant Secretary for Children and Families of the Department of Health and Human Services to make grants for kinship navigator programs
Amends part E of title IV of the Social Security Act to authorize all states to opt to enter agreements to provide kinship guardianship assistance payments.
Document 10: Directs the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, the FDIC, and the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency to conduct an empirical impact study before issuing a final rule
Requires the study to include the potential impact of such Rules upon the U.S. financial services sector.

topic.name: Federal Land Management and Use
topic.description: This topic encompasses regulations and policies governing the management, access, and use of federal lands for various purposes including recreation, military operations, and environmental protection.

Document 1: Requires that federal public land be open to access and use for hunting
ensures that federal land management decisions result in no net loss of land area accessible for hunting.
Document 2: Appropriates funds for military construction
specifies restrictions and authorities regarding the use of funds appropriated in this Act.
Document 3: directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey identified federal land
requires the city to pay or reimburse the Secretary for transaction costs.
Document 4: Codifies a national security exemption from emissions regulations for excess personal property of the Department of Defense
Document 5: directs the Department of the Interior to transfer administrative jurisdiction over 520 acres of federal land
Interior shall permit cattle grazing on the acquired land according to National Park Service laws.
Document 6: establishes as the Lake Berryessa Recreation Area specified land administered by the Bureau of Reclamation
may establish guidelines and restrictions on recreational uses.
Document 7: Allows the Secretary concerned to take action as is necessary to implement and maintain the closure to the public of any road, trail, or other portion of withdrawn land
Requires the Secretary concerned to maintain a program of decontamination of contamination caused by defense-related uses.
Document 8: Withdraws certain land located in the Sloan Hills area of Clark County, Nevada, from location, entry, and patent under the mining laws.
